author: Michelle Anderson
content:
By Michelle Anderson · Mom + Blog Mama
Mom of two big kids and one snuggly farm dog. I write about real farm life, the recipes I actually cook on weeknights, and the little things that make our homestead feel like home.

What Worked For Us With Baseball's Impossible Record to Break

Okay so I wanted to chat about this one because honestly y'all have been asking. We tried it for the last few weeks and I have actual thoughts now. I'm not talking about some obscure event. I'm talking about the game itself. The number 81 is the unbreakable record. You see, Major League Baseball has a maximum number of games allowed in a season, and it's 190 games. Anything over that is considered an official forfeit. That means teams can lose no more than 109 games in a season. The Oakland Athletics came close in 2003, losing 111 games. But the record still stands. They're not going to try to

the part you'll care about

win 101 more games to break the unbreakable record. It's just not possible.

wrapping up

author: Michelle Anderson
content:
By Michelle Anderson · Mom + Blog Mama
Mom of two big kids and one snuggly farm dog. I write about real farm life, the recipes I actually cook on weeknights, and the little things that make our homestead feel like home.

Our Little Most Majors are Actually the Oldest Tournaments Routine

Okay so I wanted to chat about this one because honestly y'all have been asking. We tried it for the last few weeks and I have actual thoughts now. Most Majors might seem like just another big golf tournament, but they're actually the oldest tournaments in the sport. We're talking about the Masters, the U.S. Open, the Open Championship, and the PGA Championship. These four events have been around since the late 19th century, with the first one taking place in 1860. The Open Championship is the oldest, and it's been running continuously since then. That's over 160 years of history, folks. It's

the part you'll care about

crazy to think about how these tournaments have been shaping the course of golf for so long. And if you think about it, it's no wonder that Most Majors are still one of the most prestigious and competitive events in golf today.

author: Michelle Anderson
content:
By Michelle Anderson · Mom + Blog Mama
Mom of two big kids and one snuggly farm dog. I write about real farm life, the recipes I actually cook on weeknights, and the little things that make our homestead feel like home.

Why I Keep Coming Back To Pele Scores 77 Goals in 1963

Okay so I wanted to chat about this one because honestly y'all have been asking. We tried it for the last few weeks and I have actual thoughts now. Pele's insane goal scoring record in 1963 still stands. That year, he averaged over 2 goals per game. His team Santos scored 275 goals, a Brazilian record. Pele's dominance on the field inspired his teammates to work even harder. The result was a team that was almost unstoppable. Santos won 47 games, another Brazilian record.
